Each item is listed with its corresponding page number.

This section lists the contents of the document, including program options, program overview, cleaning and care instructions, frequently asked questions, customer service information, optional accessories, installation instructions, and electrical connection details.
This table provides a page number index for the different sections of the user manual. The left column lists the topics and the right column lists the corresponding page numbers.
The title "Contents".
The entry "Sound emission testing" with page number 74.
This dishwasher complies with current safety requirements. Inappropriate use can, however, lead to personal injury and damage to property.

To avoid the risk of accidents and damage to the dishwasher, please read the installation plan and the operating instructions carefully before it is installed and used for the first time. They contain important information on its installation, safety, use and maintenance.
Keep the installation plan and the operating instructions in a safe place and pass them on to any future owner.
Miele cannot be held liable for damage caused by non-compliance with this information.
IMPORTANT SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS
Title and safety instructions for the use of a dishwasher.  Includes points on intended use, unsuitable uses, and user capabilities.

This section contains important safety instructions related to children and the use of the appliance.  It emphasizes keeping children away from the appliance to avoid injury and suffocation.  The text also highlights the risks associated with dishwasher detergents and the importance of seeking medical attention in case of ingestion or inhalation.

Title: IMPORTANT SAFETY INSTRUCTIONS. Subtitle: Technical safety. This section contains instructions for safe installation, repair, and maintenance of the dishwasher. It emphasizes the importance of using a Miele-authorized technician and checking for damage before use. It also highlights the necessity of a proper grounding system for electrical safety and warns against placing the dishwasher near heat sources or electrical outlets.

This section details the importance of the dishwasher being connected to a grounded electrical supply via a switched socket. It stresses easy access to the power supply for safe disconnection.  It also specifies the dangers of overheating and fire risks if the dishwasher is positioned close to electrical sockets or heat-generating appliances.
This section prohibits installing the dishwasher under or next to cooktops or open fires, explaining that high radiant temperatures could damage the appliance.
Important Safety Instructions.  A list of instructions related to the safe use and installation of a dishwasher.

This text block contains important safety instructions.  It outlines the consequences of not using a Miele-authorized technician for repairs, the necessity of using original Miele parts, and the procedure for disconnecting the appliance from the electrical supply during installation, maintenance, or repair. It also emphasizes the importance of replacing a damaged connection cable with a special cable of the same type.
Proper installation. The dishwasher must be installed and connected in compliance with the installation plan.
Safety warning triangle symbol
Be careful when installing the dishwasher. Wear protective gloves to avoid injury on any sharp metal components.
The dishwasher must be correctly aligned horizontally to ensure problem-free operation.
In order to ensure stability, built-under and integrated dishwashers must only be installed under a continuous counter which is secured to adjacent cabinetry.

If you want to convert your freestanding dishwasher to a built-under ("U") model, you will need to order the appropriate conversion kit. If you remove the existing toe-kick, you must replace it with a U-model plinth. This is necessary to avoid the risk of injury caused by protruding metal parts.

The door springs must be adjusted equally on both sides. They are correctly adjusted when the door remains stationary when left half open (approx. 45° opening angle). It is also important that the door cannot fall open.
The appliance may only be used with correctly adjusted door springs.

Important Safety Instructions: Proper Use section. Contains instructions regarding the safe use of dishwashers, including warnings against using solvents, inhaling or ingesting powder detergent, leaving the door open, sitting or leaning on the door, and using incorrect detergents or salts.
Important Safety Instructions

In an appliance with a cutlery basket (depending on model), cutlery is cleaned and dried more efficiently if placed in the basket with the handles downwards. However, to avoid the risk of injury, place knives and forks with the handles upwards.

Plastic items which cannot withstand being washed in hot water, such as disposable plastic containers or plastic cutlery should not be cleaned in the dishwasher. The high temperatures in the dishwasher may cause them to melt or lose shape.

If you use the "FlexiTimer/Delay Start" option (depending on model), make sure that the dispenser is dry before adding detergent. Wipe dry if necessary. Detergent will clog if poured into a damp dispenser and may not be thoroughly dispersed.
Please observe the information given in "Technical data" regarding the capacity of the dishwasher.
Accessories
Use only genuine original Miele parts. If spare parts or accessories from other manufacturers are used, the warranty may become invalid.
Disposal of your old dishwasher

Make the door lock inoperative so that children cannot accidentally shut themselves in. Remove or destroy the locking bracket of the door lock, and make appropriate arrangements for the disposal of the appliance.
SAVE THESE INSTRUCTIONS AND PASS THEM ON TO ANY FUTURE USER

A exploded view diagram of a dishwasher showing its different components like the upper spray arm, cutlery tray, upper basket, middle spray arm, air intake for drying, lower spray arm, filter system, data plate, rinse aid reservoir, dual compartment detergent dispenser, and reservoir for dishwasher salt.

Descriptions of the dishwasher's control panel elements, including the program selection, time display, check/refill indicators, start button, short button, delay start button, and on/off button.
A note specifying that the operating instructions apply to several dishwasher models with different heights and provides the specific dimensions for each model (Standard and XXL).
Title of the document section: "Guide to the dishwasher"
Section about caring for the environment, including disposal of packaging material and old appliances.
Section about energy-saving dishwashing, with tips on maximizing efficiency and minimizing water and electricity usage.
Additional tips on energy-saving dishwashing can be found in Miele's guide to dishwashing
A figure showing how to open the dishwasher door by reaching under the door grip and pulling it.
Instructions on how to open the dishwasher door.  A warning is included about the potential for scalding water.
An illustration showing the steps to close the dishwasher door: pushing the baskets in and lifting the door upwards until it clicks.
Instructions on how to close the dishwasher door. A warning about the danger of crushing injuries is included.
A diagram illustrating the child safety lock mechanism on the dishwasher door.
Explanation of the child safety lock feature to prevent children from opening the dishwasher.
Instructions on how to lock and unlock the dishwasher door using the child safety lock slide.

Before using for the first time Water softener In order to achieve good cleaning results, the dishwasher needs to operate with soft water. Hard water results in a build-up of calcium deposits on dishware and in the dishwasher. Water supplies with a hardness level higher than 4 gr/gal (4 °d) needs to be softened. This takes place automatically in the integrated water softener. The water softener in the dishwasher is suitable for a water hardness level of up to 73 gr/gal (70 °d).

The water softener requires dishwasher salt. Depending on the water hardness level (< 22 gr/gal, or 21 °d), dishwasher salt is not required if combination tablets are being used (see "Operation - Detergent").

The dishwasher must be programmed for the water hardness in your area. Your local water supplier can tell you the water hardness level in your area. Where the water hardness fluctuates e.g. between 10 - 16 gr/gal (10 - 15 °d), always program the dishwasher to the higher value (16 gr/gal or 15 °d in this example).
It is useful to know your water hardness so that you can provide the service technician with this information in the event of any service calls.

Enter the water hardness below: gr/gal (°d) The dishwasher is programmed at the factory for a water hardness level of 1 gr/gal (1 °d). If this setting corresponds to the hardness of your water, you do not need to do anything more. However, if you have a different water hardness, you must adjust the water softener to match your water hardness.
Displaying and setting the water hardness level
Turn the dishwasher off with the button, if it is turned on.
Press the Start button and, simultaneously, switch the dishwasher on using the button.
Keep the Start button pressed for at least four seconds until the Start indicator light comes on.
If this does not happen, start the procedure from the beginning again.
Press the button twice.
The indicator light will flash twice at short intervals.
Before using for the first time
The value which has been set for the water hardness appears as a figure in the time display (see table) according to P.
P1 will start flashing in the time display. This is the factory default setting of 1°.
This table shows the relation between different units (°d, gr/gal, °f) and the time display. It has two parts, the first part goes from 1 to 20 and the second part from 21 to 70.

Before using the appliance for the first time, you require: Approx. 2 qt (2 l) of water Approx. 2 kg (4 lbs) of dishwasher salt Domestic dishwasher detergent Rinse aid formulated for domestic dishwashers Every dishwasher is tested at the factory. There will be residual water in the appliance from this test. It is not an indication that it has been used previously.

Dishwasher salt In order to achieve good cleaning results, the dishwasher needs to operate with soft water. Hard water results in a build-up of calcium deposits on dishware and in the dishwasher. Water supplies with a hardness level higher than 4 gr/gal (4 °d) needs to be softened. This takes place automatically in the integrated water softener. The water softener in the dishwasher is suitable for a water hardness level of up to 73 gr/gal (70 °d). The water softener requires dishwasher salt. Depending on the water hardness level (< 22 gr/gal or 21 °d), dishwasher salt is not required if combination tablets are being used (see "Operation - Detergent").

Before using for the first time If the water in your area is very soft and constantly lower than 5 gr/gal (5 °d) you do not have to add dishwasher salt. The prompt to add salt will be switched off automatically.
Dishwasher detergent will destroy the water softener. Do not fill the salt container with dishwasher detergent (even liquid detergent).
Use only special, coarse-grained dishwasher salt or other pure kitchen salt. Other types of salt may contain insoluble components which can damage the water softener.
Instructions on how to fill the salt reservoir for the first time. Includes information about adding water to dissolve the salt and running a cleaning cycle after filling.
An illustration showing how to fill the salt reservoir using a funnel. It shows a bag of salt being poured into the funnel, and the funnel going into the salt reservoir.

Instructions on how to fill the salt reservoir and what to do if the salt refill indicator light comes on. It also includes a warning about corrosion and instructions on how to switch the refill indicators off if you only use combination products or switch to using detergent that does not contain salt or rinse aid.
Further instructions and clarifications regarding salt refill indicator behavior after reservoir filling, based on the dishwasher's water hardness settings.
This section describes the use of rinse aid in the dishwasher, including how to fill the reservoir, the maximum capacity, and precautions to prevent over-foaming.
This image shows how to fill the rinse aid reservoir. The image shows a bottle of rinse aid being poured into the reservoir opening.
This warning advises against using detergent in the rinse aid reservoir and recommends using only rinse aid formulated for residential dishwashers.
This note specifies that rinse aid is not needed when using "Tab" detergents.
This image shows the rinse aid reservoir with the lid open, indicating how to open the lid.
This instruction describes how to open the rinse aid reservoir lid: press the button in the direction of the arrow.
This instruction states that the rinse aid should only be added until visible in the opening.
This section specifies the approximate capacity of the rinse aid reservoir: 3.7 oz. (110 ml).
This instruction explains the importance of closing the flap firmly to prevent water from entering the reservoir during a program.
This instruction advises wiping up any spilled rinse aid to prevent over-foaming during the next program.

Rinse aid indicator When the Rinse Aid indicator lights up, the rinse aid reservoir contains sufficient rinse aid for 2-3 programs only. Add rinse aid. If you only ever use combination products in your dishwasher, you can switch both refill indicators for salt and rinse aid off if you wish (see "Options - Refill indicators").

Setting the rinse aid dosage You can adjust the rinse aid dosage for best results. The rinse aid dosage is set in stages from 0 to 6. It is set to 3 at the factory. This is the recommended setting. The dosage can turn out to be higher than the set value due to the automatic adjustment of the Automatic program. If spots appear on dishware and glassware: Increase the rinse aid dosage. If clouding or smearing appears on dishware and glassware: Decrease the rinse aid dosage.

Before using for the first time Turn the dishwasher off with the button, if it is turned on. Press the Start button and, simultaneously, switch the dishwasher on using the button. Keep the Start button pressed for at least four seconds until the Start indicator light comes on. If this does not happen, start the procedure from the beginning again. Press the button 3 times. The indicator light will flash 3 times at short intervals. The set value will appear in the display after P. The flashing sequence P 3 will appear in the display. This indicates a factory default setting of 3. Use the Start button to select the setting required. Each press on the button moves you up a level. After reaching the highest value, the counter starts from the beginning again. The setting is now stored in memory. Turn the dishwasher off with the button.

Title: Loading the dishwasher. Subtitle: Tips. This section provides instructions on how to load a dishwasher, emphasizing the removal of food residues, avoiding rinsing under running water, and warnings against washing items soiled with ash, sand, wax, grease, or paint.  It also advises on proper placement of dishes and cutlery to ensure efficient cleaning and avoiding blockage of spray arms.
This section gives specific instructions on how to load different types of dishware, including hollow items, tall narrow items, and concave items, for optimal water coverage and cleaning.
This section explains that some foods may contain natural dyes that can cause plastic items to discolor in the dishwasher, and that cleaning silver cutlery can have a similar effect.

Items not recommended for dish-washing: Wooden cutlery, cutting boards and dishware or items with wooden parts; Craft items, antiques, valuable vases, and decorative glassware; Plastic items which are not heat-resistant; Copper, brass, tin and aluminum items; Colors painted over a glaze; Delicate glassware and glassware containing lead crystal. Please note: Silverware previously cleaned with a silver polish may still be damp or spotted; Silver which has been in contact with foods containing sulphur can discolor. Aluminum components (e.g., range hood grease filters) must not be cleaned with a commercial or industrial cleaning agent. Tip: When purchasing new dishware and cutlery, make sure they are dishwasher-safe.

Loading the dishwasher Glass care: Clouding may occur on glasses after frequent washing. Purchase glassware which is dishwasher-safe (e.g., Riedel glassware). Use detergents with glass protective additives. Visit "http://www.mieleglass-care.com/international/en/glasscare/english" for more information on glass care.

Safety instructions: Do not operate the dishwasher without the upper and lower baskets in place, except when running the Tall Items program.

Instructions on how to use the upper basket for small, lightweight, and delicate items such as cups, saucers, glasses, and dessert bowls.  Shallow pans or casserole dishes can also be placed in the upper basket.
Instructions on how to handle long items like soup ladles, mixing spoons, and long knives in the upper basket. They should be placed lying down across the front.
Instructions for using the adjustable cup rack. Raise the rack for tall items, or lower it and lean glasses against it or double stack smaller pieces.
Instructions on adjusting the upper basket of a dishwasher, including raising and lowering it and setting it at an angle.

Additional notes about accommodating larger plates when tilting the lower basket.

Description of the removable MultiComfort insert and its purpose.
Instructions on how to remove the MultiComfort insert.

A diagram showing how to arrange cutlery in a 3D cutlery tray.  Cutlery is shown in various orientations to fit within the tray's compartments and to allow water to drain.
Instructions on how to load a dishwasher's cutlery tray.  It emphasizes grouping similar cutlery items for easier unloading, ensuring proper water drainage, and avoiding obstruction of the spray arm.
A diagram illustrating how to adjust the height of a section within the cutlery tray to accommodate taller items, such as serving spoons.
Additional advice on the arrangement of spoon handles if they don't fit between the holders and that the side sections of the cutlery tray can be moved to fit taller items.

A short text explaining the use of the cutlery basket if required.

Instruction on using the cutlery insert supplied for heavily soiled items. The insert separates items for better water access.
Instruction on inserting cutlery with handles facing downwards and distributing them evenly.
Only use detergents formulated for domestic dishwashers.
Refer to the manufacturer's packaging as to whether Tab products are suitable for the water hardness level in your area.
Please note that the cleaning and drying results of Tab detergents can vary greatly.
For optimum cleaning and drying results, use Miele dishwasher detergent and add Miele salt and Miele rinse aid separately. See "Optional accessories."
Follow the dosage recommended by the manufacturer on the packaging.

Unless directed otherwise, use one detergent tab or add 1 1/4 - 2 tablespoons (20 - 30 ml) detergent to compartment II, depending on the level of soiling. With very heavy soiling you can also add a small amount of detergent to compartment I.
Tabs may not dissolve completely when the Express program (if available) is used.
Use only the recommended amount of detergent. Continued use of too much detergent may cause machine damage.
Dishwasher detergents can cause burning in the nose, mouth, and throat if swallowed, or inhibit breathing.

Avoid inhaling or swallowing dishwasher detergent. Keep children away from detergent. Keep detergent out of the reach of children. Keep children away from the dishwasher when the door is open. There could still be detergent residues in the dishwasher. Only add detergent just before starting the program and lock the door with the child safety lock (depending on model).
A diagram showing how to add detergent to the detergent dispenser. The diagram shows a top-down view of the dispenser with an arrow pointing down to where the detergent should be added.
A diagram showing how to add detergent to the detergent dispenser. The diagram shows a hand adding detergent to the dispenser.

Instructions on how to add detergent to the washing machine. Includes instructions on how to press the opening catch on the detergent dispenser and how to ensure the detergent packaging is properly closed after use to avoid it becoming lumpy.

Information on the dispensing aid. Compartment I holds a maximum of 2 teaspoons (10 ml) and Compartment II holds a maximum of 3.5 tablespoons (50 ml) of detergent. The marks in compartment II indicate the levels of approximately 1 1/4 and 2 tablespoons (20 and 30 ml). The marks indicate the approximate fill level in ml when the door is open in a horizontal position.

Instructions on how to turn on the dishwasher, including ensuring spray arms are not obstructed, closing the door, turning on the water supply, and turning on the dishwasher using the button.  It also describes the behavior of the Start indicator light.

Instructions on how to select a program based on load type and soiling. It references the Program Overview chapter for details on different programs.  It explains the use of the program selection button and the indicator lights associated with program selection. It also notes that program duration will be displayed and allows for selecting program options.
A box containing additional instructions to select program options, with a reference to the "Options" section for more details.

Instructions on how to start a program in a dishwasher.  It mentions that the door must be closed and to press the Start button. It also notes that canceling a program after the first few minutes may omit important program stages.

Explanation of the dishwasher's time display. The duration of the selected program is shown in hours and minutes before it starts, and the remaining time is shown during the program.  It explains that the displayed time can vary depending on factors such as water temperature, detergent type, amount of dishes, and level of soiling.
Details about the initial display of program time. When a program is selected for the first time, the display will show an average time for a cold water intake.
Information on how the program time is calculated. The electronic module determines the program duration based on water temperature and the amount of dishes.

This section describes the energy management feature of the dishwasher, explaining how it automatically switches off to save energy after a certain period of inactivity.  It also notes that the dishwasher will remain on while a program is running, during a delay start, or if there is a fault.

This section details the process at the end of a dishwasher program. It explains that the program completion is indicated on the display and that the drying fan may run for a short time afterwards.  It also indicates that the dishwasher can then be opened and unloaded.
This section provides safety instructions to prevent steam damage to the counter. It recommends keeping the dishwasher door closed until the dishes are cool enough to prevent damage.
Instructions on how to turn off the dishwasher, including turning it off during a program or delay start time, and how to close the water supply if it will not be used for an extended time.
Instructions on how to unload the dishwasher, emphasizing the importance of allowing dishes to cool and the proper order of unloading baskets to prevent damage.

Instructions on interrupting a program.  A program will be interrupted if the door is opened. To continue, close the door and press the Start button.  A warning is given that the water may be hot and to exercise caution when opening the door. After opening, leave the door ajar for approximately 20 seconds before closing and pressing Start.

Instructions on changing a program. If the detergent dispenser flap is open, do not change the program. If a program has already started and you wish to change it, turn the dishwasher off using the button ①, then turn it back on with the same button, and select the desired program.
Description of the "Short" option, its effects on program duration and consumption rates, and its interaction with the Rapid program.  Includes step-by-step instructions for using the option.

Options Turning off the refill indicators If you consistently use a Tab detergent, you can deactivate the refill indicators if you wish. Turn the dishwasher off with the button, if it is turned on. Press the Start button and, simultaneously, switch the dishwasher on using the button. Keep the Start button pressed for at least four seconds until the Start indicator light comes on. If this does not happen, start the procedure from the beginning again.

Press the button 9 times. The indicator light will flash 9 times at short intervals. The flashing sequence in the time display will show whether the refill indicators are switched on or off. P 1: Refill indicators activated P 0: Refill indicators deactivated Press the Start button to change the setting. The setting is now stored in memory. Turn the dishwasher off with the button.
If you switch to using detergent that does not contain salt or rinse aid, it is important to remember to add dishwasher salt and rinse aid and to switch the indicators back on.
Instructions on how to use the Delay Start function of a dishwasher.  It explains how to set the delay time, the importance of a dry detergent compartment, and safety precautions for children.
A warning to prevent children from contacting dishwasher detergent.  It advises adding detergent immediately before starting the program and locking the door with the child safety lock.

Instructions and explanations of the options available for the dishwasher. This section details how to turn on the dishwasher, select a program, set the delay start time, and use the start button. It also explains how the indicator lights function.
Instructions on how to use the Optimize Standby feature, including how the display will behave and how to resume the display.
Instructions on what to do if the door is opened during the delay start time.
Instructions on how to start a program before the delay start time has elapsed.
Explanation of how the delay start time is shown in the display, with examples for different durations.

Optimize Standby section: This section explains the "Optimize Standby" feature, which automatically switches off the dishwasher after a few minutes to save energy. It also details how to activate, deactivate and change the duration of the standby time.

Options section: This section provides instructions on how to activate or deactivate the Optimize Standby feature using the button and the Start button.  It describes the indicator light behavior and the memory storage of the setting.
Options
Factory default settings
If you have altered any settings from the factory default, you can reset them back to the factory default settings as follows:
Turn the dishwasher off with the button, if it is turned on.
Press the Start button and, simultaneously, switch the dishwasher on using the button.
Keep the Start button pressed for at least four seconds until the Start indicator light comes on.
If this does not happen, start the procedure from the beginning again.

Press the button 19 times.The indicator light will flash once at a long interval and then 9 times at short intervals.The flashing sequence in the display indicates whether a setting has been changed from its factory default.
P 1: All settings are set to factory default settings P 0: At least one setting has been changed
To reset the dishwasher to the factory default settings, press the Start button.The setting is now stored in memory.Turn the dishwasher off with the button.

A table outlining different dishwasher programs, their usage, and the program sequence.  It includes columns for Program, When to use, Pre-wash, Main wash, Interim rinse, Final rinse, and Drying, detailing the steps and conditions for each program.

Check and clean your dishwasher regularly. This will help avoid problems and faults. All surfaces are susceptible to scratching. Contact with unsuitable cleaning agents can alter or discolor all surfaces.

Generally, the wash cabinet is self cleaning if the correct amount of detergent is used. If, however, there are lime scale or grease deposits in the cabinet, these can be removed with a special dishwasher cleaner. Follow the instructions on the packaging. If you mainly use low temperature programs (< 120°F/50 °C), there is a risk of bacteria and unpleasant odors building up in the wash cabinet. To prevent this happening the dishwasher will automatically increase the temperature in the final rinse of the selected program after several programs using low temperatures have been run. Clean the filters in the wash cabinet regularly.

Bacteria could build up on the door seal and the sides of the dishwasher door as these surfaces are outside the wash cabinet and are not accessed and cleaned by the spray arm jets. Wipe the door seals regularly with a damp cloth to remove food deposits. Wipe off any food or drink residues which may have dripped onto the sides of the dishwasher door before closing it.
Instructions on cleaning the door front of an appliance.  It emphasizes removing any soiling immediately and cautions against letting glass cleaners puddle on the surface.

A list of cleaning products and materials to avoid using on the appliance. This includes those containing soda, ammonia, acid, or chloride; descaling agents; abrasive cleaning products; cleaners containing solvents; stainless steel cleaners; dishwasher cleaner; oven cleaners; glass cleaners; hard, abrasive brushes or sponges; eraser sponges; sharp metal scrapers; steel wool; and steam cleaning appliances.
The filter system in the base of the wash cabinet prevents food from being redeposited on dishes. It also protects the circulation pump from damage caused by foreign objects.
This dishwasher must not be used without all the filters in place!
Collection of coarse soil may cause the filters to clog. The level of soiling and time it takes before the filters need cleaning will vary depending on use.
Check the condition of the filters regularly and clean them if necessary.
A diagram showing how to remove the filter system from the dishwasher.
Turn the dishwasher off.
Turn the handle counter-clockwise to release the filter system 1.
Lift the filter system out of the dishwasher 2. Remove any coarse particles and rinse the filter well under running water. Use a nylon brush if necessary.
Ensure that course soil does not get into the circulation system and cause a blockage.

A diagram showing how to clean the inside of a filter by pressing catches together in the direction of arrows and opening the filter.  The diagram includes numbered arrows and details of the filter's components.

Instructions for replacing the filter system. It involves ensuring the system lies flat, turning the handle clockwise until arrows point towards each other (locking it in place), and a warning to ensure correct filter system fitting.

Frequently Asked Questions With the help of the following guide, minor faults in the performance of the appliance, some of which may result from incorrect operation, can be put right without contacting Miele Service. This will save time and money. The following guide may help you to find the reason for a fault, and to correct it. You should, however, note the following: Unauthorized or incorrect repairs could cause personal injury or damage to the appliance. Repair work to electrical appliances must only be carried out by a suitably qualified and competent person in strict accordance with current local and national safety regulations. The manufacturer cannot be held liable for unauthorized work.

Subtitle: Water intake fault

Table describing the problem and possible causes and solutions for water intake fault. It has two columns: Problem and Possible cause and solution. The Problem column describes issues related to the intake indicator flashing and the dishwasher stopping during a program. The Possible cause and solution column offers solutions like turning on the water supply, checking the water intake, and checking water pressure.
Subtitle: Water drainage fault

Table describing the problem and possible causes and solutions for water drainage fault. It has two columns: Problem and Possible cause and solution. The Problem column describes issues related to the dishwasher stopping and the indicator flashing during a program. The Possible cause and solution column offers solutions such as turning the dishwasher off, checking for water in the wash cabinet, cleaning the filter system, cleaning the drain pump, cleaning the non-return valve, and removing any kinks in the drain hose.

A table with two columns and three rows. The first column lists the problem (noise). The second column lists the possible cause and the solution.

A table with two columns and multiple rows. The left column lists problems related to poor cleaning results in a dishwasher, while the right column offers possible causes and solutions for each problem.  The problems include dishes not being clean, smearing on glassware and cutlery, and a bluish sheen on glassware. Solutions involve checking dish loading, selecting a more intensive program, using enough detergent, rearranging items to allow spray arms to rotate freely, cleaning the filter system, cleaning spray arm jets, and adjusting rinse aid dosage.

The left column lists various problems related to a dishwasher, such as dishware not drying properly, discoloration of glasses, and stains not being removed. The right column provides possible causes and solutions for each problem.  The table is organized to provide troubleshooting guidance for dishwasher issues.

A table with two columns and multiple rows. The first column lists problems encountered with a dishwasher, such as white residue on dishes and rust stains on cutlery. The second column provides possible causes and solutions for each problem.

Instructions on cleaning the drain pump and non-return valve of a Miele dishwasher.  Includes steps for turning off the dishwasher, removing the filter system, scooping out water, pressing the catch for the non-return valve, lifting and rinsing the valve, and removing foreign objects.
Diagram showing the location of the drain pump and non-return valve within the dishwasher.  Arrows indicate parts mentioned in the text instructions.

Explanation that the drain pump is located beneath the non-return valve.  Additional instructions to remove foreign objects from the drain pump and ensure the non-return valve is not blocked before reinserting.

MieleCare (USA only)
MieleCare, our Extended Service Contract program, gives you the assurance of knowing that your appliance investment is covered by 5 years of worry free ownership.

MieleCare is the only Extended Service Contract in the industry that guarantees repairs by a Miele Authorized Service Provider using genuine Miele parts. Only genuine Miele parts installed by factory trained professionals can guarantee the safety, reliability, and longevity of your Miele appliance.
Please note that unless expressly approved in writing by Miele's Service department, Extended Service Contracts offered by other providers for Miele products will not be recognized by Miele. Our goal is to prevent unauthorized (and untrained) service personnel from working on your Miele products, possibly doing further damage to them, you and/or your home.
To learn more about MieleCare Extended Service Contracts, please contact your appliance dealer or visit us online at:
http://www.mieleusa.com

Please refer to the Installation Diagram supplied with these instructions. To comply with IEC sound testing protocols, please contact Miele for additional instructions.
Information is subject to change. Please refer to our website to obtain the most current product specification, technical & warranty information.

Installation, repairs and other work by unqualified persons could be dangerous. Check that the voltage, frequency and fuse rating shown on the data plate match the household mains supply. The power outlet must be accessible after installation. Do not use an extension cord to connect the dishwasher to the mains. Danger of overheating! See data plate on the right side of the door. A damaged power cord must only be replaced with a genuine Miele cord by a Miele service technician.

Improper connection of the equipment grounding conductor can result in electric shock. Do not modify the plug provided with the appliance; if it will not fit the outlet, have a proper outlet installed by a qualified electrician. If there is any question concerning the proper electrical connection or grounding of this appliance to the powersupply, please consult a licensed electrician or call Miele's Technical Service Department: USA: 1-800-999-1360 CDN: 1-800-565-6435

THIS APPLIANCE MUST BE GROUNDED. In the event of a malfunction or breakdown, grounding will reduce the risk of electric shock by providing a path of least resistance for the electrical current. This appliance is equipped with a cord having an equipment grounding conductor and a grounding plug. The plug must be plugged into an appropriate outlet that is installed and grounded in accordance with all local codes and ordinances.
Specifications The dishwasher comes equipped with a 4 ft (1.2 m) powercord with a molded plug for connection to 120 V, 60 Hz, 15 A grounded receptacle.

Hardwired electrical connection.  Instructions for hardwiring the appliance to an existing power cable, including a caution about disconnecting the power supply before servicing and the importance of qualified technicians for installations and repairs.  Emphasizes the need to match voltage and frequency and to consult an electrician if there are any doubts.
Grounding instructions. This section details the requirements for grounding the appliance, emphasizing the connection to a grounded metal, permanent wiring system or an equipment-grounding conductor.

Electrical connection.  Instructions on making a hardwire connection to the terminal, including the importance of removing the power cord to prevent electric shock.  Specifies the minimum thickness of the supply cord or cable and the fuse rating. Details the wiring connections for live, neutral and ground wires.

Diagram showing the wiring connections to the terminal block.  Labels are visible for Ground (G), Neutral (N), and Live (L). The diagram shows a cable connected to the terminal block with screws securing the connection.
Water connection
Water intake
Water in the dishwasher must not be used as drinking water.
The dishwasher may be connected to a cold or hot water supply up to a maximum of 140°F/60°C.

We would only recommend connection to a hot water supply if it is economical, e.g., a solar-powered supply. When connected to a hot water supply, all program stages which would otherwise be carried out with cold water will be carried out with hot water.
The intake hose is approx. 4' 11"/1.5 m long. A 1.5 m long flexible metal extension hose (pressure tested to 2,030 psi) is available as an optional accessory, if required.
A faucet with a 3/8" male thread must be provided on site.

This appliance is constructed to comply with ANSI/ASSE 1001 // IEC/EN/DIN 61770 // VDE 0700 Part 600, and may be connected to a suitable supply without an extra non-return valve if national regulations permit.
The water pressure (flow pressure at the take-off point) must be between 4.35 - 145 psi. If the water pressure is too high, a pressure reducer valve must be fitted.
After connecting the dishwasher to the water supply, check that the water intake is airtight.
To avoid any damage to the appliance, the dishwasher must only be connected to fully vented pipework.
The intake hose must not be shortened or damaged in any way as it contains electrical components.
This text block contains instructions on how to connect the water supply to the dishwasher and how to ensure that there are no leaks. It also includes instructions on how to vent the drain.
This text block describes the drainage system of the dishwasher, including the non-return valve, the length of the drain hose, and how to connect it to the drain.
This image shows how to connect the drain hose to the dishwasher and to the drain pipe.

This text block provides additional instructions on how to connect the drain hose if it is to be directly fitted to the drainage outlet. It also explains how to direct the hose to the left or right of the appliance and how to adapt the on-site connector to different widths of hose.
